title: HVAC&R Design Team Early Involvement
summary: The HVAC&R design team should provide input during the earliest phases of a project, as these are most critical to project success.
evidence_quote: The HV AC&R design team should strive to provide input during the earlie st phases (when HV AC&R design input has historically been minimal) since these are the most critical to project success, as they set the stage for all subsequent work.

title: Design Intent and Criteria Pairing
summary: Each design intent must be paired with a design criterion that provides a benchmark for minimum acceptable performance.
evidence_quote: Each design intent must be paired with a design criterion , which provides a benchmark for minimum acceptable performance rela- tive to the intent.

title: Design Validation Importance
summary: Design validation is essential to confirm that a chosen design option meets the design criteria, connecting design intent and decisions.
evidence_quote: Design validation is esse ntial to successful design; other- wise there is no connection betwee n design intent and design deci- sions.

title: Design vs. Analysis Distinction
summary: Design problems may have multiple acceptable solutions, unlike analysis problems which have a unique correct solution.
evidence_quote: A design problem may or may not be completely defined (some of the parameters may be missing) and th ere are any number of potentially acceptable answers.

title: Iterative Design Process
summary: Design is an iterative process where initial assumptions are made and improved through subsequent analysis.
evidence_quote: To overcome this obstacle, make informed initial assumptions and improve on them through subse- quent analysis.

title: Building Program Content for HVAC Design
summary: The building program should include client objectives, space functions, occupant and equipment patterns, site location, codes, budgets, and schedule.
evidence_quote: The building program should include, but need not be limited to, the following: ... The client’s objectives and strategies for the initial and future functional use of the building...

title: Owner's Project Requirements Documentation
summary: The building program and HVAC&R systems program should be explicitly documented as the Owner's Project Requirements (OPR) per ASHRAE Guideline 0-2005.
evidence_quote: This documentation is termed the Owner’s Project Requirements (OPR) by ASHRAE Guideline 0-2005, and it pro- vides the context for all design decisions.

title: HVAC&R System Program Information
summary: The HVAC&R system program should include design outdoor temperatures, degree-days, wind velocity, applicable codes, and utility characteristics.
evidence_quote: The information that should be contained in the HV AC&R sys- tem program includes ... design outdoor dry-bulb and wet-bulb temperatures (absolute and coincident);

title: Environmental Conditions Definition
summary: Environmental conditions for each space should be defined by dry-bulb and wet-bulb temperatures during occupied and unoccupied hours, ventilation requirements, and special conditions.
evidence_quote: The environmental conditions to be maintained for each build- ing space should be defined by ... dry-bulb and wet-bulb temperatures during daytime occupied hours, nighttime occupied hours, and unoccupied hours;

title: Schematic Design System Evaluation Criteria
summary: During schematic design, evaluate HVAC&R systems for space requirements, energy use, costs, acoustics, compatibility, and indoor air quality.
evidence_quote: For each system cons idered during this phase, eval- uate the relative space (and volume) requirements for equipment, ducts, and piping; fuel and/or electrical use and thermal storage requirements; initial and life-cycle costs; acoustical requirements and capabilities; compatibility with the building plan and the struc- tural system; and the effects on indoor air quality, illumination, and aesthetics.

title: Impact of Envelope and Internal Loads on HVAC
summary: The HVAC designer may need to evaluate building envelope options, heavy lighting loads (>2 W/ft²), and unusual internal loads (>4 W/ft²) on system performance.
evidence_quote: the HV AC&R designer may be asked to provide an evaluation of the impact of building envelope design options ... heavy lighting loads (i.e. , more than 2 W/ft2 [22 W/m2]), and other unusual internal loads (i.e., more than 4 W/ft2 [43 W/m2]) on HV AC system performance and requirements.

title: Coordination of HVAC with Other Disciplines
summary: Close integration of mechanical and electrical systems with structure, plan, and building configuration requires cooperation of all team members.
evidence_quote: Close integration of the mechanical and electrical systems with structure, plan, and building configuration requires th e cooperation of all team mem- bers—architect, mechanical engineer, electrical engineer, structural engineer, acoustical consultant, and professionals from other disci- plines.

title: Energy Code Compliance Considerations
summary: State or local building or energy codes may restrict glass area, envelope assemblies, lighting, HVAC systems, and annual energy budget.
evidence_quote: The requirements of state or lo cal building or energy codes (see Section 2.7) must also be considered at this point because of restric- tions on the amount of glass, other envelope assembly require- ments, lighting, HV AC&R system and equipment limitations, and, in some instances, on the annual building energy budget.

title: Design Development: System Sizing and Energy Studies
summary: In design development, heat loss/gain and ventilation calculations are refined to size air/water distribution systems and select equipment for design and part-load conditions.
evidence_quote: Heat loss, heat gain, and ventilation calculations are refined and used to design the air and water distribution systems and to select equipment. System sizes and capacities are selected to ma tch design and part-load condi- tions.

title: Design Documentation and Approval
summary: Design decisions should be clearly documented, whether they constitute approval of or deviation from the designer's proposals.
evidence_quote: Design de cisions should be clearly docu- mented whether they constitute appr oval of, or deviation from, the designer's proposals.
